It operates on group expressions containing CTE anchor operators and reduces memo depth by eliminating the anchor layer, allowing the CTE producer to be directly connected to consumers.

summary: This rule derives and pushes down new filter predicates to the children of a Logical Join based on the join ON clause and domain properties (statistics/constraints). It infers constraints on one side of the join from the other side using equi-join or range-join conditions, effectively pruning data early in the execution plan.
ra_condition: θ contains binary equal/range predicates, JoinType ∉ {FULL_OUTER, CROSS}, φ derived from domain properties
correctness_conditions: Join type must not be Full Outer Join or Cross Join
Derived predicates must be logically implied by the join condition combined with the domain properties of the opposite side
For Outer Joins, predicates are only pushed to the preserved-null side's counterpart (e.g., Left Outer Join pushes to Right child)
For Anti Joins, predicates are pushed to the probe side (Right child for Left Anti Join)
performance_impact: Beneficial when derived predicates significantly reduce cardinality early, enabling partition pruning or index usage. Potentially negative if derivation overhead exceeds pruning benefits or if filters are redundant with existing constraints.
edge_cases: Queries with NULL values in join columns may limit derivation effectiveness
Non-equi joins (e.g., <, >) are supported only if classified as range predicates
Constant predicates in ON clause are explicitly skipped
Schemas with missing or inaccurate column statistics (DomainProperty) may yield no derived predicates
framework_notes: Matches LOGICAL_JOIN with PATTERN_LEAF children in the Cascades memo. Creates LogicalFilterOperator nodes. Does not trigger Enforcer insertion directly but relies on DomainProperty cached in OptExpression. Controlled by session variable 'enable_predicate_move_around'.

summary: This rule optimizes correlated subqueries by restructuring the LogicalApply operator's right child. It splits filter predicates beneath an aggregation into correlated and uncorrelated parts, pushing uncorrelated predicates below the aggregation to reduce data early, while pulling correlated predicates above the aggregation and adjusting grouping keys to maintain semantic correctness.
ra_condition: p_c contains only binary equality predicates referencing outer columns, G is empty or Apply is existential
correctness_conditions: Correlated predicates must be binary equality operators
Aggregation must have no grouping keys unless the Apply operator is existential (EXISTS/NOT EXISTS)
At least one correlated predicate must exist in the filter
Grouping keys must be expanded to include columns referenced by rewritten correlated predicates
performance_impact: Beneficial when uncorrelated predicates are selective, significantly reducing the number of rows processed by the aggregation. May incur overhead if expanding grouping keys increases aggregation cardinality or memory usage unnecessarily.
edge_cases: Correlated predicates using non-equality operators (e.g., >, <) are not supported
Aggregations with existing grouping keys in non-existential subqueries are excluded
Queries where no correlated predicates are found in the filter will throw an exception
framework_notes: Implemented as an RBO rule in the Cascades framework. Matches LOGICAL_APPLY patterns with specific child structures (Aggregation over Filter). It modifies the logical plan within Memo groups without explicit Enforcer insertion, relying on subsequent rules to handle physical implementation.

summary: Pushes filter predicates down through a project operator, separating deterministic and non-deterministic predicates to enable earlier filtering while maintaining semantic correctness
ra_output_pattern: π_c(σ_p'(R)) with optional σ_p''(π_c(σ_p'(R))) if non-deterministic predicates exist
ra_condition: p contains deterministic predicates that reference project columns computed from deterministic expressions
correctness_conditions: Predicates must be deterministic functions to ensure consistent results
Project columns referenced by predicates cannot be computed using non-deterministic functions
No ASSERT_TRUE functions in project column expressions
Lambda functions must be enabled via session variable if present in project expressions
performance_impact: Beneficial when filter reduces significant rows before expensive projection computation; reduces I/O and CPU by filtering early; may have minimal impact if projection is cheap or filter selectivity is low
edge_cases: All predicates are non-deterministic (no pushdown occurs, returns empty list)
Project contains lambda functions with enableLambdaPushDown disabled
Project contains ASSERT_TRUE function calls (check method returns false)
Predicates reference columns computed with non-deterministic expressions (those predicates stay above project)
framework_notes: Cascades RBO rule matching LogicalFilter over LogicalProject pattern in Memo; separates deterministic/non-deterministic predicates into separate filter operators; applies ScalarOperatorRewriter with PROJECT_REWRITE_PREDICATE_RULE list after ReplaceColumnRefRewriter transforms column references

summary: This rule pushes filter predicates down through a Union operator, applying the filter to each branch of the union individually to reduce data volume early in the execution plan.
ra_condition: Columns referenced in predicate p exist in all union inputs R and S
correctness_conditions: Predicate columns must be semantically equivalent across all union branches
Predicate evaluation must not have side effects
NULL handling must remain consistent between pre-union and post-union filtering
performance_impact: Beneficial by reducing data volume early and enabling partition pruning or index scans in base tables. Potentially negative if predicate is computationally expensive and duplicated across many branches with small data volumes.
edge_cases: Unions with a large number of branches cause predicate duplication overhead
Predicates involving columns that are not present or aligned in all union branches
Cases where evaluating the predicate once on merged data is cheaper than multiple times on subsets
framework_notes: Matches LOGICAL_FILTER over LOGICAL_UNION in the Memo. Delegates transformation logic to PushDownPredicateSetRule. Creates new OptExpressions for each union child, triggering further exploration of pushed-down paths.

summary: Pushes filter predicates from a LogicalFilter operator down into supported LogicalScan operators, combining them with existing scan predicates to enable storage-level filtering and pruning.
ra_condition: Scan operator type ∈ SUPPORT set (e.g., OLAP, HIVE, ICEBERG)
correctness_conditions: Predicate columns must be available in the scan output schema
Predicate evaluation must be deterministic and side-effect free
Combined predicate must be semantically equivalent to original filter sequence
Null handling must be preserved during predicate simplification
performance_impact: Highly beneficial for reducing I/O by enabling partition pruning, index filtering, and early data elimination at the storage engine. Minimal planning overhead.
edge_cases: Scan types not included in the SUPPORT set (e.g., custom scans)
Predicates containing UDFs not executable within the storage engine
Predicates relying on runtime state not accessible during scan initialization
Complex predicates that might increase CPU cost at scan layer without reducing I/O
framework_notes: Matches LOGICAL_FILTER with children in SUPPORT set. Combines predicates using Utils.compoundAnd and simplifies them. Returns an identity Project wrapping the new Scan to maintain output column structure. Does not trigger physical enforcers directly.

summary: Prunes unused output columns from a Logical Union operator based on parent requirements, updating child column mappings to ensure only necessary data is processed and passed up.
ra_condition: C = Parent_Required_Columns ∩ Union_Output_Columns, |C| ≥ 1
correctness_conditions: All children must produce columns compatible with the pruned schema types
At least one column must remain in the output schema to prevent empty relation errors
Positional mapping between Union outputs and child outputs must be preserved for remaining columns
performance_impact: Reduces memory footprint and network shuffle size by eliminating unused columns early. Optimization overhead is negligible as it involves simple set operations.
edge_cases: SELECT * queries where all columns are required (no pruning occurs)
Aggregates like COUNT(*) where no specific columns are required (rule forces one column to remain)
Children with duplicate column expressions requiring offset-based pruning instead of ID-based
framework_notes: Matches LOGICAL_UNION with PATTERN_MULTI_LEAF. Uses OptimizerContext TaskContext to retrieve required columns. Modifies LogicalUnionOperator child output mappings. Ensures minimum 1 column output. Does not trigger Enforcer insertion directly.

summary: This rule pushes a LogicalApply operator below a LogicalProject operator on its left input branch, effectively removing the intermediate Project if the underlying child provides all columns required by the Apply's predicate, specifically for uncorrelated scalar subqueries.
ra_condition: PredicateCols(Apply) ⊆ OutputCols(L) AND Subquery is Uncorrelated Scalar
correctness_conditions: The subquery must be an uncorrelated scalar subquery (verified by check method).
All columns referenced by the Apply operator's predicate (outerColumns) must be present in the output of the left child's input (L).
The outer Project's column mapping must be reconstructable over the new Apply output schema.
The transformation must preserve the semantic output schema of the original plan.
performance_impact: Generally beneficial as it reduces operator count and plan depth, potentially enabling further optimizations on the left input. It is unlikely to hurt performance because the check ensures no required columns are lost, and the Apply operator's column requirements remain unchanged.
edge_cases: Correlated subqueries are excluded by the check method.
Transformation is skipped if the left child (L) does not contain all columns required by the Apply predicate.
If the intermediate Project performed significant column pruning for downstream operators not present in this subtree, benefits might be localized.
framework_notes: Implemented as a Cascades RBO rule (RuleType.TF_PUSH_DOWN_APPLY). It matches LogicalApply patterns in the Memo structure. It does not trigger Physical Enforcer insertion as it transforms Logical to Logical. It simplifies the expression tree to facilitate further rule matching.

summary: This rule pushes a LogicalProject operator below a LogicalApply operator on the right branch. It rewrites the Apply's correlation predicates and subquery operators to reference the Project's input columns instead of its output columns, then places a new Project on top to maintain the original output schema.
ra_condition: θ references columns produced by π_{map}, and π_{map} can be inverted to reference R columns
correctness_conditions: The Apply operator must represent a correlated subquery (checked via SubqueryUtils)
All column references from the right side in the Apply's predicate and subquery operator must be resolvable through the Project's column reference map
The new top Project must reconstruct the exact output schema of the original Apply operator
The transformation must preserve the semantic equivalence of the correlation condition
performance_impact: Beneficial when it enables further pushdown of the Apply operator into the right child's tree (e.g., below Joins or Aggregates), allowing earlier filtering. Potentially negative if the Project computed expensive expressions that are now duplicated or if the new top Project prevents early column pruning upstream.
edge_cases: Non-correlated subqueries are skipped by the check method
Projects with complex expressions may increase predicate complexity in the Apply operator
If the Project removes columns required by the Apply without mapping them, the rewrite would fail (though typically prevented by planner validity)
Redundant if the Apply does not reference any columns from the Project's output
framework_notes: Matches LOGICAL_APPLY with LOGICAL_PROJECT as the right child. Uses ReplaceColumnRefRewriter to adjust column references. It is an RBO rule that modifies the Memo by adding new OptExpressions. It does not trigger Physical Enforcers as it operates on Logical operators.

summary: Optimizes global MIN/MAX aggregations on partition columns by rewriting the plan to scan only relevant partitions or retrieve values from partition metadata, avoiding full table scans.
ra_condition: f ∈ {MIN, MAX}, c ∈ PartitionColumns(T), GroupBy = ∅
correctness_conditions: Session variable enable_rewrite_partition_column_minmax is enabled
Table is a partitioned OLAP table (Native or MV) with ≤ 1 partition column
Aggregation contains only MIN/MAX functions referencing the partition column
No GROUP BY keys
No HAVING predicate on aggregation
No LIMIT on the scan operator
No specific partition names or tablet/replica hints in the scan
For partition prune optimization: Table must be DUP_KEYS without deletions and no scan predicates
performance_impact: Drastically reduces I/O and CPU by avoiding full table scans; converts data scan to metadata lookup or single partition scan.
edge_cases: Unique Key or Aggregate Key tables (partition prune path restricted to DUP_KEYS)
Tables with row deletions enabled
Queries with WHERE clauses on the scan
Tables with multiple partition columns
Queries with LIMIT clauses
Queries specifying specific partition names or tablet IDs
framework_notes: RBO rule matching LOGICAL_AGGR -> LOGICAL_PROJECT -> LOGICAL_OLAP_SCAN pattern. Operates on OptExpressions in the Memo.

summary: This rule removes the LogicalCTEAnchor operator from the execution plan after the Common Table Expression (CTE) has been inlined into the main query body, effectively cleaning up the plan tree.
ra_condition: context.getCteContext().needInline(CTE_ID) is true
correctness_conditions: All references to the CTE within the Consumer plan must have been substituted with the Producer definition
The CTE must not be recursive
The transformation must preserve the semantic output of the Consumer
performance_impact: Beneficial: Reduces plan tree depth and operator overhead by removing unnecessary wrapper nodes. No direct execution cost change as logic is already inlined.
edge_cases: Recursive CTEs which require materialization or special handling
CTEs referenced multiple times where inlining causes plan bloat
CTEs explicitly marked for materialization by the optimizer
framework_notes: Matches LogicalCTEAnchorOperator groups in the Memo. RBO rule that simplifies the logical tree. Does not trigger physical property enforcers. Reduces group expression complexity.

summary: This rule infers new filter predicates on join inputs by analyzing the join ON clause and the domain properties (statistics/constraints) of the opposite input, allowing early filtering to reduce cardinality before the join operation.
ra_condition: JoinType ∉ {Full Outer, Cross}, q contains binary equal/range predicates linking R and S, domain properties available
correctness_conditions: Join type must permit filtering on specific sides without altering null-supply semantics (e.g., Inner allows both, Outer allows non-preserved side filtering)
Derived predicates must be logically implied by the conjunction of the join predicate and the opposite side's domain constraints
Full Outer Joins are excluded to avoid eliminating rows that require null-extension on either side
Predicates must be binary equality or range operators to allow valid domain intersection and derivation
performance_impact: Beneficial when derived predicates are selective, enabling partition pruning, index usage, or reducing shuffle size. Overhead is low if domain properties are cached, but transformation is skipped if domains are unknown or too broad to yield new constraints.
edge_cases: Full Outer Joins (explicitly disabled in check method)
Cross Joins (no ON predicate to analyze)
Join predicates involving complex expressions or non-binary operators
Cases where DomainProperty is missing or represents the entire data range (no pruning possible)
framework_notes: Matches LogicalJoin group expressions with two children in the Cascades Memo. Relies on DomainProperty attached to child OptExpressions. Generates LogicalFilter operators around children without removing the original ON predicate. Does not trigger Enforcer insertion directly; standard transformation rule behavior applies.

summary: This rule transforms a LogicalApply operator where the right child is an Aggregation containing a Filter. It pushes the Aggregation below the Filter by adding correlation columns to the Aggregation's grouping keys, allowing the Filter to be applied on the Aggregation's output.
ra_condition: P contains correlation predicates, all correlation predicates are binary equality, G is empty or Apply is existential
correctness_conditions: The subquery must be correlated (references outer columns)
Correlation predicates must be binary equality operators (=)
Aggregation must have no grouping keys OR the Apply operator must be existential (EXISTS/NOT EXISTS)
Correlation columns must be added to aggregation grouping keys to preserve semantics
performance_impact: Beneficial: Prepares correlated subqueries for unnesting into Joins, avoiding expensive per-row nested loop execution. Hurt: Minimal, as Apply execution is generally inferior to Join execution for correlated subqueries.
edge_cases: Correlation predicates using non-equality operators (e.g., >, <) will cause transformation failure
Aggregated subqueries with existing grouping keys that are not existential subqueries
Subqueries without any correlation predicates in the filter
framework_notes: Matches OptExpression patterns in the Memo tree. This is a Logical equivalence rule (RBO) that restructures the tree to enable subsequent Apply-to-Join transformation rules. It does not directly insert Physical Enforcers.

summary: Prunes empty constant inputs from a Logical EXCEPT operation. If the first input is empty, the result is empty. If subsequent inputs are empty, they are removed. If only one input remains, the EXCEPT is replaced by a projection.
ra_output_pattern: ∅ (if E_1=∅), E_1 − ... − E_n (excluding empty E_k where k>1), or π(E_remaining) (if single input remains)
ra_condition: At least one E_i is an empty relation (∅)
correctness_conditions: The EXCEPT operator must not have an attached projection (input.getOp().getProjection() == null)
Empty inputs must be LogicalValuesOperator instances containing zero rows
Output column schema must be preserved via projection if the EXCEPT operator is eliminated
performance_impact: Beneficial: Reduces plan complexity and execution cost by short-circuiting empty results or removing redundant set difference operations. No known negative performance impact.
edge_cases: All inputs empty: Returns empty LogicalValuesOperator
Only left input non-empty: Converts to LogicalProjectOperator to match EXCEPT output schema
Left input empty: Short-circuits to empty result regardless of other inputs
framework_notes: Matches LOGICAL_EXCEPT with PATTERN_MULTI_LEAF. Implemented as a Transformation Rule (TF_PRUNE_EMPTY_EXCEPT). Simplifies logical plan before physical optimization. Does not trigger enforcers directly.

summary: Rewrites a Logical TopN operator over an Olap Scan to utilize vector search indexes when the sorting order is based on a vector distance function, pushing the distance calculation into the scan operator.
ra_condition: Vector index exists on v, dist matches index metric, k > 0, single order by column, vector feature enabled
correctness_conditions: Experimental vector feature must be enabled
Scan operator must have a projection
TopN limit must be greater than 0
TopN must have exactly one order by element
Order by expression must match a supported vector distance function
Query vector dimension must match index dimension
Existing predicates must be convertible to vector range filters
performance_impact: Significantly improves performance for K-Nearest Neighbor queries by using Approximate Nearest Neighbor (ANN) indexes instead of full table scan and sort; IVFPQ index types bypass this optimization due to accuracy concerns.
edge_cases: IVFPQ index types skip the rewrite
Predicates not convertible to vector range cause rule failure
Multiple order by columns prevent optimization
Dimension mismatch between query vector and index throws exception
Scan without projection cannot be optimized
framework_notes: Matches LOGICAL_TOPN with LOGICAL_OLAP_SCAN child. Modifies LogicalOlapScanOperator to include VectorSearchOptions and a virtual distance column. Does not trigger physical enforcers directly but prepares logical plan for vector execution.

summary: This rule normalizes COUNT aggregation results within a Scalar Apply operator to ensure semantic correctness. It replaces COUNT column references with IFNULL(column, 0) to handle cases where the Apply implementation produces NULLs for non-matching rows, enforcing the SQL standard that COUNT returns 0 for empty sets.
ra_condition: Apply is Scalar, Aggregation contains COUNT, Subquery expression references COUNT columns
correctness_conditions: The Apply operator must be a Scalar Apply (returns single value per outer row)
The inner side must be an Aggregation containing COUNT functions
The Apply implementation produces NULL for non-matching inner rows (e.g., via Left Join)
COUNT semantics require 0 instead of NULL when no rows match
No nested correlation subqueries exist within the input tree
performance_impact: Negligible CPU overhead for IFNULL evaluation. Primarily ensures correctness rather than performance gain. Prevents potential query result errors.
edge_cases: Non-scalar Apply operators (e.g., IN/EXISTS subqueries)
Aggregations without COUNT functions
COUNT columns not referenced by the Apply subquery expression
Queries with nested correlated subqueries inside the aggregation
framework_notes: Matches LOGICAL_APPLY with LOGICAL_AGGR child in StarRocks Cascades optimizer. RBO rule that rewrites operator expressions without changing the logical tree structure significantly. Does not trigger physical property enforcers.

summary: Propagates column requirements from the filter predicate and parent operator down to the child leaf operator to enable column pruning during exploration.
ra_condition: R is a leaf operator (e.g., LogicalScan) and context column requirements are updated
correctness_conditions: Columns referenced in filter predicate p must be preserved in R's output
Columns required by the parent of the filter must be preserved in R's output
No side-effecting functions in predicate p depend on pruned columns
performance_impact: Significantly reduces I/O and memory usage by preventing child operators from reading or processing columns not needed by the filter or parent.
edge_cases: Filter predicate references all available columns (no pruning possible)
Child operator is not a leaf (e.g., Join, Aggregate) - handled by other rules
Complex UDFs in filter that obscure column dependencies
framework_notes: Modifies OptimizerContext TaskContext required columns directly; returns empty list as it constrains child exploration rather than generating alternative logical plans; matches Filter with Leaf child group expressions.

summary: This rule transforms a Logical Join above a Logical Aggregation into a Logical Aggregation above a Logical Join, effectively pushing the Join operator below the Aggregation on the right branch. This allows join predicates to be evaluated before aggregation, potentially filtering data earlier.
ra_condition: R is unique (contains PK), Join is Inner/LeftOuter, Agg is Global, No COUNT(*)
correctness_conditions: Left input (R) must be unique (contain all primary keys) to prevent duplicating Right rows which would invalidate aggregates
Join type must be Inner or Left Outer to ensure Right rows are not duplicated or null-extended in a way that breaks aggregation
Aggregation must be Global and not Split to ensure single-phase aggregation semantics
Aggregation cannot contain COUNT(*) as it depends on row count which changes if Join filters rows
Predicates referencing aggregated columns must be moved to the new Aggregation operator
performance_impact: Beneficial when join predicates are highly selective on the Right table, allowing data reduction before expensive aggregation. Potentially harmful if the Join produces significant intermediate data that the Aggregation would have otherwise reduced prior to the Join.
edge_cases: Queries involving tables not in the hardcoded MOCK_TPCH_PRIMARY_KEYS map will not trigger this rule (uniqueness check fails)
Aggregations containing COUNT(*) are excluded
Split or Local Aggregations are excluded
Right Outer or Full Outer Joins are excluded
Left side must trace down to LogicalOlapScan to verify primary keys
framework_notes: Implemented in StarRocks Cascades optimizer using OptExpression pattern matching. Matches LogicalJoin with LogicalAggr as right child. Transforms logical plan and returns new OptExpression list for Memo insertion. Relies on ColumnRefFactory for column management and ScalarEquivalenceExtractor for predicate handling.

summary: This rule acts as a composite container that groups multiple logical transformation rules together. It matches input expressions based on operator type and delegates the actual transformation logic to its bundled sub-rules via the predecessorRules mechanism, optimizing rule matching overhead.
ra_condition: Operator type of E is in the allowed set (or set is empty)
correctness_conditions: All bundled sub-rules must preserve semantic equivalence
Operator type filtering must accurately reflect sub-rule requirements
Sub-rules must be safe to apply in the order determined by the optimizer engine
performance_impact: Beneficial for reducing pattern matching overhead by batching rules with similar operator constraints; may introduce slight indirection overhead but generally improves optimizer throughput by grouping related transformations.
edge_cases: Queries with operator types not in the 'ops' set will bypass all bundled rules
If 'ops' is empty, it matches all operators which may trigger unnecessary checks for sub-rules
Recursive rule application might need careful ordering to avoid infinite loops if sub-rules regenerate same patterns
framework_notes: Does not directly insert Enforcers (Logical rule). Matches GroupExpressions in the Memo based on OperatorType. Controls explore depth indirectly by batching rules; the empty transform method implies the framework iterates predecessorRules for actual transformations.

summary: Transforms a single COUNT DISTINCT aggregation into a 4-stage aggregation plan with intermediate bucketing to mitigate data skew on the distinct column.
ra_output_pattern: γ_{G, SUM}(γ_{G, SUM}(γ_{G, B, COUNT(DISTINCT D)}(γ_{G, D}(R))))
ra_condition: Aggregation is COUNT DISTINCT AND (Skew Hint Present OR Session Bucketization Enabled)
correctness_conditions: Bucket column B is deterministically derived from distinct column D (hash(D) % N)
Specific value of D always maps to the same bucket B across all nodes
SUM of partial COUNT DISTINCT per bucket equals total COUNT DISTINCT
Intermediate deduplication (G, D) prevents duplicate counting before bucketing
performance_impact: Beneficial when distinct column values are highly skewed, preventing single-node bottlenecks. Detrimental on uniform data due to extra shuffle and aggregation stages (4 stages vs standard 2-3 stages).
edge_cases: Small data volumes where overhead exceeds skew cost
Low cardinality distinct columns where skew is minimal
Incorrect bucketNum configuration (too low to resolve skew, too high adding overhead)
Queries without GROUP BY columns (global count distinct)
framework_notes: Matches LOGICAL_AGGR with leaf child. Generates 4 OptExpression layers. Sets DataSkewInfo with cost factors (1.0 for stage 1, 0.5 for stage 3/4) to influence CostModel. AggType.GLOBAL implies required data redistribution (Enforcer/Exchange).

summary: This rule optimizes Logical EXCEPT operations by pruning empty constant inputs. If the first input is empty, the result is empty. If subsequent inputs are empty, they are removed. If only one input remains, the EXCEPT operator is replaced by a Project operator to maintain schema compatibility.
ra_output_pattern: ∅ (if R_1=∅), R_1 − ... − R_n \ {R_i} (if R_i=∅, i>1), π(R_remaining) (if single input remains)
correctness_conditions: The EXCEPT operator must not have an embedded projection (input.getOp().getProjection() == null)
Empty inputs must be logically equivalent to an empty set (LogicalValuesOperator with 0 rows)
Remaining inputs must be projected to match the original EXCEPT output schema if the EXCEPT operator is removed
Set difference semantics must be preserved (A − ∅ = A, ∅ − B = ∅)
performance_impact: Beneficial by reducing the number of inputs to the set operation, potentially short-circuiting execution to return empty immediately if the left side is empty, and avoiding unnecessary difference computations against empty sets.
edge_cases: EXCEPT operator with an embedded projection (rule precondition fails)
All inputs are empty (returns empty Values operator)
Single non-empty input remains (requires schema alignment via Project operator)
Column type mismatches between inputs (assumed valid by optimizer prior to this rule)
framework_notes: Implemented as an RBO TransformationRule in the Cascades framework. Matches LOGICAL_EXCEPT with PATTERN_MULTI_LEAF. Operates on OptExpressions in the Memo. Does not trigger Enforcer insertion but reduces plan complexity.

summary: This rule transforms a LogicalUnion operator whose children are LogicalValues operators (containing constants) into a single LogicalValues operator. If only some children are mergeable Values, it merges them into one Values operator and keeps the rest in a simplified Union.
ra_input_pattern: ∪(V₁, V₂, ..., Vₙ) where Vᵢ are Values operators
ra_condition: Vᵢ contains only constant scalars, no limit, no predicate
correctness_conditions: Children must be LogicalValues operators
Children must not have limits or predicates (unless all children are merged)
Scalar expressions in Values rows must be constants
Column types and nullability must be compatible across union branches
performance_impact: Beneficial: Reduces plan complexity by collapsing multiple Values scans into one. Reduces overhead of Union operator execution. Unlikely to hurt performance as Values are in-memory and cheap.
edge_cases: Union branches containing non-constant expressions
Union branches with existing limits or predicates
Mixed Union of Values and table scan operators
Union DISTINCT (rule assumes UNION ALL semantics for merging)
framework_notes: RBO rule matching LOGICAL_UNION with PATTERN_MULTI_LEAF children. Operates on OptExpression in Cascades Memo. Does not trigger physical Enforcer insertion. Reduces expression tree depth by eliminating Union nodes when possible.

summary: Transforms a LogicalTopN operator ordering by a vector distance function over a LogicalOlapScan into a plan that utilizes Approximate Nearest Neighbor (ANN) index search, pushing distance calculation into the scan operator.
ra_condition: f is a supported vector distance function, v has a vector index, dim(q) == dim(index), enable_experimental_vector is true
correctness_conditions: Vector index dimension must match the query vector dimension
TopN must order by exactly one vector distance function expression
Predicates on the vector column must be convertible to a distance range filter or be absent
TopN limit must be greater than zero
Scan operator must have a defined projection
performance_impact: Significantly reduces latency for K-Nearest Neighbor queries by leveraging ANN indexes instead of brute-force scanning and sorting; may introduce overhead for small tables or when high recall is required (e.g., IVFPQ handling).
edge_cases: IVFPQ index types skip the distance column rewrite due to accuracy requirements requiring brute-force refinement
Complex predicates on the vector column that cannot be parsed to a range value cause the rule to abort
TopN operators with multiple order-by elements are not supported
Queries without vector indexes on the scanned table will not match
framework_notes: RBO rule in StarRocks Cascades framework; matches LogicalTopN with LogicalOlapScan child; modifies LogicalOlapScanOperator metadata to enable ANN search and injects a virtual distance column into the schema; adds alternative expression to Memo for cost-based selection.

summary: This rule optimizes queries by pushing a TopN operator below the preserved side of an Outer Join (Left or Right). It duplicates the TopN operator, placing one below the join to reduce input cardinality while keeping the original TopN above the join to ensure correct final results.
ra_condition: order ⊆ columns(R), Join ∈ {⟕, ⟖}, Predicate = ∅, Limit < Threshold
correctness_conditions: Join predicate must be empty to ensure no rows from the preserved side are filtered out before the join
Order By columns must exclusively reference columns from the preserved side of the outer join
TopN operator must not contain an offset
The preserved side child must not already have a tighter limit
performance_impact: Beneficial when the preserved side table is large and the limit is small, drastically reducing join cost. Potentially negative if the limit is large, adding unnecessary sort overhead without significant cardinality reduction.
edge_cases: TopN with offset is not supported
Joins with explicit join predicates are excluded
Joins with user-specified hints are excluded
Order columns referencing the null-supplying side are excluded
Limits exceeding the session variable threshold are excluded
framework_notes: Implemented as a Logical-Logical transformation in the Cascades framework. It matches a LogicalTopN atop a LogicalJoin. It does not remove the original TopN but inserts a new one below the join. It relies on session variables to cap the pushdown limit.

summary: This rule normalizes COUNT aggregate results within a scalar Apply operator by wrapping column references with IFNULL(..., 0), ensuring COUNT semantics (never NULL) are preserved in subquery expressions.
ra_condition: Apply is scalar, γ contains COUNT, Apply logic references COUNT columns
correctness_conditions: COUNT aggregate function semantically never returns NULL (returns 0 for empty sets)
Scalar subquery must return single row or NULL
If Aggregation produces a row, COUNT columns must be 0 not NULL when input is empty
performance_impact: Negligible CPU overhead for IFNULL call; enables downstream optimizations relying on non-null COUNT values; prevents incorrect predicate evaluation due to NULL propagation.
edge_cases: Non-scalar Apply operators (e.g., EXISTS, IN)
Nested correlation subqueries within the Apply
COUNT columns not referenced in Apply's subquery operator
Aggregations without COUNT functions
framework_notes: Matches Pattern(LOGICAL_APPLY, PATTERN_LEAF, LOGICAL_AGGR). RBO rule running in Cascades optimizer. Rewrites LogicalApply operator's internal subquery operator without changing input children. Does not trigger Enforcer insertion.

summary: This rule normalizes Logical Repeat operators within CTE Produce structures by adding an explicit identity projection if one is missing, ensuring consistent column handling for recursive queries.
ra_condition: Repeat operator projection is null
correctness_conditions: The identity projection must include all columns from the Repeat operator's child output info
The identity projection must include all columns specified in Repeat's output grouping
Column references must remain semantically equivalent (identity mapping)
The Repeat operator's schema must remain unchanged after projection addition
performance_impact: Generally neutral; normalizes the plan to enable subsequent projection-based optimizations like column pruning, with negligible overhead.
edge_cases: Recursive CTEs where the Repeat operator already defines a projection
Schemas where column output info does not align with repeat grouping columns
Queries without CTEs or recursive structures
framework_notes: Matches LOGICAL_CTE_PRODUCE with child LOGICAL_REPEAT in the Memo. Modifies the LogicalRepeatOperator in-place within the OptExpression. Does not trigger Enforcer insertion as it does not enforce physical properties. Facilitates downstream rules requiring explicit projections.

summary: This rule prunes tablet IDs for OLAP scan operations by either using hint tablet IDs or computing optimal tablet selection through partition pruning, reducing the number of tablets that need to be scanned during query execution.
ra_condition: Hint tablet IDs exist OR partition pruning can eliminate irrelevant tablets
correctness_conditions: Hint tablet IDs must be valid and exist in the target table
Partition pruning must not eliminate tablets containing data matching query predicates
Selected tablet IDs must be a subset of originally available tablets
Query semantics and result set must be preserved after tablet reduction
Selected partition ID must be correctly identified before tablet pruning
performance_impact: Beneficial when it reduces I/O and network overhead by scanning fewer tablets, especially for partitioned tables with selective predicates. Minimal impact if all tablets must be scanned anyway or if pruning overhead exceeds scan savings.
edge_cases: Empty hint tablet IDs list falls back to partition-based pruning
No partition predicates available for pruning logic
Full table scan scenarios where all tablets must be accessed
Hint tablet IDs reference non-existent or inaccessible tablets
Result equals original selected tablet IDs (no transformation needed)
framework_notes: Matches LOGICAL_OLAP_SCAN operators in StarRocks Cascades framework. Does not trigger enforcer insertion as it's a logical equivalence transformation (RBO). Returns empty list if no change needed, controlling memo exploration by avoiding redundant group expressions. Pattern created with OperatorType.LOGICAL_OLAP_SCAN.

summary: This rule propagates column requirements through a Logical Filter operator during the column pruning phase. It updates the optimizer context to ensure the child operator produces all columns needed by the filter's predicate in addition to columns required by the parent, enabling downstream column pruning without changing the logical plan structure.
ra_condition: Propagate required columns (Parent_Required ∪ Predicate_Required) to child R
correctness_conditions: The filter predicate semantics must remain unchanged
All columns referenced in the filter predicate must be included in the child's output schema
All columns required by the parent operator must be included in the child's output schema
The filter operator must pass through all input columns that satisfy the predicate
performance_impact: Beneficial: Significantly reduces I/O, memory footprint, and network shuffle traffic by preventing unnecessary columns from being read or processed upstream. Optimization overhead is negligible as it only updates context metadata.
edge_cases: Filters with constant predicates (no column references) still propagate parent requirements
Filters on top of operators that cannot prune columns (e.g., SELECT *)
Queries where reading full rows is more efficient due to vectorization or storage layout
Complex predicates referencing columns not present in the child schema (validation error)
framework_notes: Returns an empty list, indicating it modifies the OptimizerContext (TaskContext) rather than generating new plan alternatives. It relies on the Cascades framework to re-explore the child group expressions with the updated required columns. Matches LogicalFilter with any child operator (PATTERN_LEAF).

summary: This rule optimizes queries filtering on ranking window function results by deriving a limit from the predicate and inserting a PartitionTopN operator below the Window operator, enabling more efficient execution in the pipeline engine.
ra_condition: Pipeline engine enabled, window function is ranking type, predicate is comparison on window result column
correctness_conditions: Pipeline engine must be enabled in session variables
Window operator must be a rank-related analytic operator (e.g., row_number, rank)
Predicate must be a binary comparison (LE, LT, EQ) on the window output column against a constant
If multiple window operators exist, sort columns must align to avoid negative gain from extra enforcers
Pre-agg optimization settings must be respected for stacked window operators
performance_impact: Beneficial when limiting large datasets early reduces shuffle and computation cost via PartitionTopN; potentially harmful if inserted TopN introduces extra sorting or enforcing between stacked window operators with mismatched sort columns
edge_cases: Non-pipeline execution engine
Non-ranking window functions (e.g., sum, avg, lead)
Predicates not matching rank <= N pattern (e.g., rank > N, complex expressions)
Multiple window operators with conflicting sort requirements
Empty partition by columns with non-row_number topN type
framework_notes: Matches LogicalFilter -> LogicalWindow pattern in Cascades; checks session variables for pipeline engine and pre-agg settings; may trigger Enforcer insertion if sort columns differ between consecutive window operators; transforms LogicalFilter to include LogicalTopN below LogicalWindow

summary: Transforms a Join over Aggregation into an Aggregation over Join, pushing the aggregation operator below the join when the left input is unique on join keys to allow earlier filtering.
ra_condition: θ is INNER or LEFT OUTER JOIN, R is unique on join keys, γ does not contain COUNT(*)
correctness_conditions: Left input (R) must contain all primary keys to ensure uniqueness on join keys and prevent row duplication
Join type must be Inner or Left Outer to preserve right input cardinality semantics for aggregation
Aggregation must be Global and not Split to ensure single-phase correctness
Aggregation cannot contain COUNT(*) to avoid semantic drift with nulls introduced by joins
performance_impact: Beneficial when join predicates filter significant data from S before aggregation, reducing grouping cardinality and I/O. Detrimental if join produces large intermediate results, increasing aggregation cost, memory usage, and network shuffle.
edge_cases: Queries using COUNT(*) in aggregation
Left table lacking primary key or unique constraint
Right Outer or Full Outer Joins
Distributed query plans where aggregation is already split
Left side is not a base table scan with visible primary keys
framework_notes: Matches LogicalJoin with LogicalAggr on right child in Cascades memo. Uses ColumnRefFactory for column mapping and ScalarEquivalenceExtractor for predicate handling. Transforms logical expressions without immediate physical enforcement.

summary: This rule matches Logical CTE Consume operators to extract and record consumption metadata (CTE ID, limits, predicates) into the optimizer context, enabling downstream CTE optimizations such as predicate pushdown and inlining.
ra_condition: CTE ID is registered in CTEContext and column mapping is valid
correctness_conditions: Column reference mapping between CTE consume and produce operators must be bijective and accurate
Predicate rewriting must preserve semantic equivalence relative to the CTE output schema
Aggregated limits and predicates from multiple consume sites must be correctly merged in context
performance_impact: Enables significant runtime performance gains by allowing filters and limits to push down into CTE definitions, reducing intermediate data volume; compilation overhead is negligible.
edge_cases: CTEs referenced multiple times require correct aggregation of constraints from all consume sites
Recursive CTEs may have limitations on pushdown capabilities
CTEs with complex column aliasing require precise rewriter configuration
framework_notes: Matches LOGICAL_CTE_CONSUME pattern in the Memo. Updates OptimizerContext side-effectively without generating new OptExpressions (returns empty list). Runs during exploration to inform CTE Producer optimization decisions.

summary: Eliminates constant columns from the GROUP BY clause when an aggregation is preceded by a projection, reducing grouping cardinality while preserving semantics.
ra_condition: Columns C in grouping keys map to constants in Map, and |K ∪ C| > 1
correctness_conditions: At least one grouping key must remain if all original keys are constant to preserve empty-input row count semantics
Constants must be deterministic and side-effect free
Session variable isEnableConstantExecuteInFE must be true
performance_impact: Beneficial: Reduces hash table size and shuffle keys for aggregation. Overhead: Adds projection operators, usually negligible.
edge_cases: Single grouping key (rule disabled to prevent semantic change on empty input)
All grouping keys are constant (one key retained in aggregation)
Aggregation predicates (HAVING) referencing constant group keys are rewritten
framework_notes: Matches LOGICAL_AGGR over LOGICAL_PROJECT pattern. RBO rule in Cascades framework. Does not trigger physical enforcers directly but simplifies logical plan for downstream costing.

summary: This class functions as a meta-rule container that groups multiple transformation rules to optimize the rule matching process within the Cascades framework, delegating actual query transformations to its contained sub-rules rather than modifying the expression tree itself.
ra_condition: Input operator type matches registered sub-rule types or sub-rule types are unspecified
correctness_conditions: Semantics are preserved by delegation to contained rules
Contained rules must individually maintain logical equivalence
No state is modified by the CombinationRule itself
performance_impact: Improves optimizer compilation and matching throughput by reducing the number of top-level rule iterations; has no direct impact on query execution performance.
edge_cases: Queries with operator types not registered in the 'ops' set will bypass this rule group
Dynamic rules without fixed patterns will match all operators if 'ops' remains empty
framework_notes: The transform method returns an empty list, indicating the actual logic resides in rules returned by predecessorRules(); the optimizer engine likely expands this rule into its sub-rules during the fix-point iteration.

summary: This rule transforms a single GROUP BY COUNT(DISTINCT) aggregation into a four-stage aggregation plan with intermediate bucketing to mitigate data skew on the distinct column.
ra_output_pattern: γ_{G, SUM}(γ_{G, SUM}(γ_{G, B, COUNT(DISTINCT D)}(γ_{G, D}(R) ⊳ B)))
ra_condition: Operator is LogicalAggregation with COUNT(DISTINCT) AND (SkewHint OR Session.EnableDistinctColumnBucketization)
correctness_conditions: The bucketing function (hash(D) % N) must be deterministic so identical D values map to the same bucket B.
Duplicate (G, D) pairs must be eliminated in the first stage to prevent counting inflation.
The sum of distinct counts across all buckets must equal the global distinct count (valid because buckets partition the value space).
The bucket column type must be wide enough to hold the bucket number (TINYINT or SMALLINT).
performance_impact: Beneficial when the distinct column has high skew (heavy hitters), as it distributes load across multiple reducers. Detrimental for uniform distributions or small datasets due to added aggregation stages (4 vs 1-3) and extra network shuffles (2 vs 1).
edge_cases: Low cardinality distinct columns where skew is negligible.
Small data volumes where overhead exceeds skew mitigation benefits.
Bucket number configuration too small (ineffective skew reduction) or too large (excessive overhead).
Queries without session variable enabled and without explicit skew hint.
framework_notes: RBO rule matching LOGICAL_AGGR with leaf child. Generates 4 OptExpressions (LogicalAggregation). Uses DataSkewInfo to adjust cost factors (1.0 for stage 1, 0.5 for stage 3-4). Relies on AggType (LOCAL/GLOBAL) to trigger Exchange operators downstream rather than inserting Enforcers directly.

summary: This rule identifies logical aggregation operators containing COUNT(DISTINCT ...) functions applied to Bitmap type columns. It rewrites these specific aggregations to use the BITMAP_UNION_COUNT function instead. This transformation leverages StarRocks' specialized bitmap processing capabilities for better performance.
ra_condition: type(c) = BITMAP ∧ arity = 1
correctness_conditions: The aggregated column must be of BITMAP type
The aggregation function must be COUNT with DISTINCT modifier
The aggregation must have exactly one argument
BITMAP_UNION_COUNT must be semantically equivalent to COUNT(DISTINCT) for BITMAP types in this context
performance_impact: Significant performance improvement by using native bitmap union operations instead of generic hashing for distinct counting. No negative impact expected when type constraints are met.
edge_cases: Columns not of BITMAP type
COUNT DISTINCT with multiple arguments
Non-distinct COUNT aggregations
Aggregations where distinct bitmap objects counting is intended rather than cardinality union
framework_notes: Matches LOGICAL_AGGR operators in the Memo. Replaces the LogicalAggregationOperator with a new instance forcing AggType.GLOBAL. Does not trigger physical property enforcers directly.

summary: Pushes filter predicates down through join operators to reduce data early in the query execution plan by evaluating predicates closer to base tables
ra_output_pattern: σ_p1(R) ⋈_q σ_p2(S) where p ≡ p1 ∧ p2
ra_condition: Predicate p references join input columns; for OUTER JOIN requires isEnableJoinPredicatePushDown()=true
correctness_conditions: Predicate must reference only columns from join input relations
For INNER JOIN: predicates can be pushed to either or both sides
For OUTER JOIN: predicates on null-supplying side must preserve NULL semantics
Join predicate pushdown feature must be enabled in optimizer context for outer joins
Predicate evaluation must not change result cardinality or NULL behavior
performance_impact: Beneficial when predicates are selective and reduce join input size significantly; may hurt if predicate evaluation cost exceeds join reduction benefit or if statistics are inaccurate
edge_cases: Outer joins with predicates on null-producing side requiring special NULL handling
Predicates referencing columns from both join sides (cannot be fully pushed)
When context.isEnableJoinPredicatePushDown() returns false for outer joins
Correlated predicates or subqueries within filter conditions
Predicates involving volatile or non-deterministic functions
framework_notes: Matches LogicalFilter->LogicalJoin pattern in Cascades memo structure; uses JoinPredicatePushdown helper class for predicate distribution; does not trigger enforcer insertion; optimization controlled via OptimizerContext flags; operates on OptExpression tree in memo groups

summary: Pushes filter predicates down through join operators to reduce data volume before joining, improving query performance by early filtering on individual join inputs.
ra_output_pattern: σ_p1(R) ⋈_q σ_p2(S) or σ_p1(R) ⋈_q S or R ⋈_q σ_p2(S)
ra_condition: Predicate p references columns from one or both join inputs; for OUTER JOIN requires context.isEnableJoinPredicatePushDown() = true
correctness_conditions: For INNER JOIN: predicate can be safely pushed to either or both sides without changing semantics
For OUTER JOIN: predicate can only be pushed to the preserving side (side that doesn't receive null-extended rows)
Predicate must reference columns from only one side to push to that specific side
Join predicate pushdown must be enabled in optimizer context for outer joins
Predicate evaluation must not have side effects that depend on join order
performance_impact: Beneficial when predicates are highly selective and reduce input cardinality before expensive join operations. Reduces memory usage and I/O by filtering early. May have minimal impact if predicates are not selective or if pushdown requires duplicate evaluation on both sides of the join.
edge_cases: Outer joins with predicates on the null-supplying side cannot be pushed down safely without changing semantics
Predicates referencing columns from both join inputs cannot be pushed down to either side individually
When context.isEnableJoinPredicatePushDown() is false for outer joins, rule is disabled
Complex predicates with subqueries or UDFs that require full join output for correct evaluation
Predicates that reference columns modified by the join condition itself
framework_notes: Matches LOGICAL_FILTER over LOGICAL_JOIN pattern in Cascades Memo groups. Uses JoinPredicatePushdown helper class to analyze predicate column references and determine valid pushdown targets. Does not trigger enforcer insertion as it operates on logical expressions only. Transformation creates new OptExpression(s) that replace the original filter-join pattern in the Memo. Rule is part of RBO (Rule-Based Optimization) phase with logical equivalence transformation type.

summary: Merges multiple constant LogicalValues children of a LogicalUnion into a single LogicalValues operator to simplify the plan.
ra_input_pattern: ∪(V₁, V₂, ..., Rₙ) where V₁, V₂ are constant value tables
ra_condition: At least two children are LogicalValues containing only constants, without local limits or predicates
correctness_conditions: Union semantics must allow row reordering (typically UNION ALL)
Merged columns must have compatible data types and nullability
Constant expressions must be side-effect free
Original Union limit and predicate must be preserved on the new operator
performance_impact: Reduces plan node count and execution overhead by consolidating constant data scans into a single operator.
edge_cases: Non-constant Values operators (e.g., containing functions) are not merged
Values operators with local Limits or Predicates are excluded
If Union requires DISTINCT, deduplication logic must be preserved downstream
Mixed children (Tables + Values) result in a partial merge retaining the Union operator
framework_notes: RBO rule (TF_MERGE_CONSTANT_UNION). Matches LOGICAL_UNION with PATTERN_MULTI_LEAF children. Generates new ColumnRefOperators to prevent ID conflicts in the Memo.

summary: This rule rewrites a logical aggregation containing COUNT DISTINCT (and other supported aggregates) into a two-stage aggregation plan (local followed by global) to optimize distributed execution, or simplifies it if the distinct column is a primary key.
ra_condition: A contains single-column DISTINCT aggregates on column D, R is Hash Distributed, and (G ∪ {D}) covers R's distribution keys
correctness_conditions: All distinct aggregates must reference exactly one common column
The distinct column must be a direct column reference, not an expression
Non-distinct aggregates must be decomposable across two stages (e.g., SUM, MAX, MIN)
The underlying scan operator must use Hash Distribution specification
The combination of Group By keys and the distinct column must cover all shuffle columns of the scan
The aggregation operator must not have a Limit clause applied
performance_impact: Significantly reduces network shuffle traffic for high-cardinality distinct counts by pre-aggregating locally; adds CPU overhead for the extra aggregation stage which may be negligible for large data but costly for small datasets.
edge_cases: Queries with multiple distinct columns in the same aggregation
Distinct functions applied to expressions instead of raw columns
Aggregate functions not present in the support mapping (e.g., AVG without decomposition)
Aggregation queries containing a LIMIT clause
Tables with Random or Broadcast distribution specifications
Cases where the distinct column is a Primary Key (triggers single-stage optimization instead)
framework_notes: Matches LOGICAL_AGGR over LOGICAL_OLAP_SCAN patterns. Explicitly checks HashDistributionSpec rather than relying on Enforcers. Transforms a single OptExpression into a chain of two Aggregation operators (Local + Global) unless PK optimization applies.

summary: Merges two consecutive Logical Filter operators into a single Filter operator by combining their predicates using logical AND, reducing plan complexity and operator overhead.
ra_condition: None (transformation is always semantically valid for standard SQL predicates)
correctness_conditions: Predicates are side-effect free and deterministic
Logical AND associativity and commutativity hold for the predicate logic
NULL handling semantics remain consistent under conjunction
performance_impact: Generally beneficial by reducing operator overhead and tree depth. May enable further optimizations like predicate pushdown. No significant negative performance cases expected.
edge_cases: Volatile functions in predicates may have evaluation order changed
Redundant predicates are deduplicated via Set
Does not trigger if filters are separated by other operators
framework_notes: Logical transformation rule; does not trigger Enforcer insertion. Reduces expression tree depth in Memo by collapsing two levels. Matches Pattern: LOGICAL_FILTER -> LOGICAL_FILTER -> LEAF.

summary: Pushes a Logical TopN operator below a Logical Outer Join operator onto the preserved side of the join to reduce cardinality before joining.
ra_condition: order columns ⊆ output(R), JoinType ∈ {LeftOuter, RightOuter}, No Offset, No Join Predicate
correctness_conditions: TopN must have a limit and no offset
Limit must be within session threshold (CboPushDownTopNLimit)
Join type must be Left Outer or Right Outer
Join operator must not have a join hint
Join operator must not have an additional predicate
Order by columns must all originate from the preserved side of the join
Preserved side child must not already have a tighter or equal limit
performance_impact: Beneficial when the preserved side is large and the limit is small, significantly reducing join input size. Potentially harmful if the added sort cost outweighs join savings or if the limit is large.
edge_cases: Full Outer Joins are not supported
Inner Joins are not supported by this specific rule
Queries with TopN offset are excluded
Joins with explicit hints or non-equi predicates are excluded
Ordering columns referencing the null-supplying side prevent pushdown
framework_notes: Matches Pattern(LOGICAL_TOPN -> LOGICAL_JOIN). Creates new OptExpressions in Memo. Does not remove the original TopN, preserving final result limiting. Logical rule, does not directly insert physical enforcers.

summary: This rule prunes unused output columns from a Logical CTE Consume operator based on the columns required by the parent operator and local predicates, ensuring only necessary data is propagated.
ra_condition: C_req ⊆ C_all AND (C_all - C_req) ∉ UsedColumns
correctness_conditions: Pruned columns are not referenced by the parent operator
Pruned columns are not used in the CTE consume's local predicate
At least one column is retained to maintain valid relation semantics
performance_impact: Reduces memory footprint and data transmission by eliminating unused columns from CTE output; negligible cost unless it prevents CTE sharing across branches requiring different column sets.
edge_cases: Queries selecting no columns (e.g., EXISTS) force retention of one column
CTEs referenced multiple times with different column requirements may limit sharing benefits
framework_notes: Matches LogicalCTEConsumeOperator patterns; updates column reference mapping; ensures non-empty output projection to avoid invalid plans; does not trigger physical enforcers.

summary: This rule rewrites a logical aggregation containing COUNT DISTINCT into a two-stage aggregation (local followed by global) to optimize distributed execution, provided the distinct column and grouping keys satisfy specific data distribution requirements.
ra_condition: F contains distinct aggregates on single column D, R is hash distributed, Shuffle(R) ⊆ (G ∪ {D}), Shuffle(R) ⊈ G
correctness_conditions: All distinct aggregates must operate on the same single column reference.
Non-distinct aggregates must support two-phase decomposition (e.g., SUM, MAX, COUNT).
The underlying scan operator must use Hash Distribution specification.
The union of grouping keys and the distinct column must cover all shuffle columns of the scan.
The grouping keys alone must not already cover all shuffle columns (rule targets cases where distinct column aids distribution alignment).
The aggregation operator must not have a Limit clause.
performance_impact: Beneficial for reducing network shuffle traffic and memory usage during high-cardinality distinct counts by pre-aggregating locally. May introduce CPU overhead for small datasets or simple aggregates due to the additional aggregation stage.
edge_cases: Queries with multiple distinct columns in the same aggregation.
Distinct functions applied to expressions rather than raw column references.
Aggregate functions not present in the supported transformation maps (e.g., AVG without explicit decomposition).
Scan nodes using Random or Replicated distribution strategies.
Aggregation operators with Limit clauses.
framework_notes: Matches LOGICAL_AGGR over LOGICAL_OLAP_SCAN in the Cascades framework. Transforms a single OptExpression into a chain of two aggregations using ColumnRefFactory for intermediate outputs. Explicitly checks DistributionSpec in the check phase to ensure shuffle correctness.

summary: Ensures LogicalRepeat operators within CTE Produce contexts have an explicit identity projection if missing, facilitating column pruning and schema normalization for recursive CTEs.
ra_condition: Repeat.projection is null
correctness_conditions: Identity projection maps all columns to themselves preserving semantics
Projection includes all output columns from Repeat's child
Projection includes all grouping columns required by Repeat operator
performance_impact: Enables downstream column pruning and predicate pushdown rules on CTE structures; negligible cost as it is a logical identity transformation.
edge_cases: No transformation occurs if LogicalRepeat already has a projection
Relies on accurate RowOutputInfo from the child operator
Specific to recursive CTE execution paths
framework_notes: Matches LOGICAL_CTE_PRODUCE parent with LOGICAL_REPEAT child pattern. Mutates the LogicalRepeat operator in-place rather than creating a new OptExpression layer. Does not trigger physical property enforcers.

summary: This rule merges two consecutive Logical Filter operators into a single Logical Filter operator by combining their predicates using logical AND.
ra_condition: None (transformation is always semantically valid for standard SQL predicates)
correctness_conditions: Predicates must be side-effect free
Logical AND commutativity holds for the predicate logic
Three-valued logic (NULL handling) remains consistent under conjunction
performance_impact: Beneficial: Reduces operator overhead, decreases plan tree depth, and simplifies predicate evaluation. Potentially improves code generation. No significant negative impact expected.
edge_cases: Predicates containing volatile functions (e.g., random()) where evaluation order might matter
Already merged predicates (idempotent due to Set usage)
Complex nested predicates that might benefit from separate normalization steps
framework_notes: Matches Pattern(LOGICAL_FILTER -> LOGICAL_FILTER). Implemented as a Transformation Rule in StarRocks Cascades. Inserts new OptExpression into Memo without triggering physical Enforcers. Reduces exploration depth by simplifying the logical tree.

summary: Prunes redundant grouping keys from a Logical Aggregation operator when they are functionally dependent on other grouping keys due to Unique Key constraints.
ra_condition: G contains Unique Key columns, D is functionally dependent on G via UK constraint, D not in required output columns
correctness_conditions: Unique Key constraints are valid and enforced on the underlying table
Removed grouping columns are functionally determined by the remaining grouping columns
Removed columns are not referenced in the required output columns of the parent task
Expressions in group by are deterministic and map to single underlying UK columns
performance_impact: Reduces the cardinality of groups, decreases shuffle data volume in distributed execution, and lowers memory consumption for hash tables during aggregation. Minimal overhead unless constraint collection is expensive.
edge_cases: Session variable isEnableUKFKOpt is disabled
No UK/FK constraints are collected or available for the tables
Group by expressions involve non-deterministic functions
Pruned columns are explicitly required by the parent operator's output projection
framework_notes: Matches pattern LogicalAggr -> LogicalProject. Relies on UKFKConstraintsCollector to populate constraints in OptExpression. Transformation rule (RBO) that modifies the LogicalAggregationOperator's grouping keys and partition columns. Does not trigger enforcer insertion.

summary: This rule optimizes LogicalOlapScanOperator by pruning the list of tablets to be scanned based on partition predicates or user hints, reducing the data scan scope before physical planning.
ra_condition: Tablets_new ⊆ Tablets_old derived from partition predicates or hints
correctness_conditions: Partition pruning logic must guarantee no relevant data partitions are excluded
User provided tablet hints must be valid and intended for execution
Table partition metadata must be consistent with the actual data distribution
performance_impact: Beneficial by reducing I/O, network traffic, and parallel task overhead. Negligible CPU cost for metadata pruning. No negative performance cases unless pruning logic is computationally expensive or incorrect.
edge_cases: Tables without partitioning yield no pruning benefits
Queries lacking partition predicates cannot prune tablets
Dynamic partitions changing during query execution may cause stale pruning
framework_notes: Matches LogicalOlapScanOperator in the Memo. It is a logical transformation rule (TF) that updates operator properties without changing the operator type. It does not trigger enforcers directly but reduces search space for physical implementation rules by refining selected tablet IDs.

summary: This rule prunes unused output columns from a Logical CTE Consume operator by analyzing the columns required by the parent operator and the local predicate, ensuring only necessary data is requested from the CTE definition.
ra_condition: req ⊆ all AND req includes all columns referenced by parent and local predicate
correctness_conditions: Pruned columns are not referenced by the parent operator
Columns used in the CTE consume predicate are preserved
At least one column is retained to maintain a valid tuple schema
performance_impact: Beneficial by reducing memory footprint and data transfer between CTE producer and consumer; no significant negative performance impact expected.
edge_cases: Queries selecting all columns (SELECT *) will not trigger pruning
CTEs consumed multiple times require coordination at the producer level for global pruning
Predicates referencing CTE columns prevent those specific columns from being pruned
framework_notes: Matches LOGICAL_CTE_CONSUME operator type; transforms OptExpression within the same logical group; includes fallback logic to ensure non-empty output mapping.

summary: Splits a LIMIT operator into global and local limits for distributed query execution, where local limit fetches limit+offset rows and global limit applies the final limit with offset.
ra_condition: limit operator is initialized (isInit() = true), R is a leaf operator
correctness_conditions: Local limit must fetch at least limit + offset rows to ensure global limit has enough data
Ordering must be preserved between local and global limit operations
Distribution strategy must support splitting limit across nodes
Both limits must operate on the same sort order if ORDER BY exists
performance_impact: Beneficial for distributed queries with LIMIT and OFFSET by reducing data transfer between nodes. May add overhead for simple single-node queries or when offset is zero since it introduces an extra operator layer.
edge_cases: Queries with OFFSET = 0 may not need splitting
Small result sets where splitting adds unnecessary complexity
Queries already optimized with pushdown limits at scan level
Single-node execution where global/local distinction is meaningless
framework_notes: Creates nested OptExpressions in Cascades memo structure. Does not trigger enforcer insertion directly. Matches LOGICAL_LIMIT operator with PATTERN_LEAF child. Rule type is TF_SPLIT_LIMIT indicating transformation rule.

summary: Optimizes ranking window queries by pushing down predicates (e.g., rank <= N) as a PartitionTopN operator below the Window operator, reducing the volume of data processed by the window function.
ra_output_pattern: σ_{w_col <= N}(ω_{P, O, func}(λ_{P, O, N}(R)))
ra_condition: w_col is output of ranking func, pipeline engine enabled, P/O match between Window and TopN
correctness_conditions: Predicate must be a binary comparison (LE, LT, EQ) on the window output column against a constant
Window function must be a rank-related analytic function (row_number, rank, dense_rank)
PartitionTopN must preserve the ordering and partitioning required by the Window function
Pipeline execution engine must be enabled in session variables
If multiple window operators exist, sort columns must align to avoid extra enforcer overhead
performance_impact: Beneficial when the limit N is significantly smaller than the partition size, reducing window computation cost. May hurt performance if the TopN operator introduces excessive sorting overhead or conflicts with existing sort properties in multi-window plans.
edge_cases: Non-pipeline execution engine
Window functions not supporting rank optimization (e.g., sum, avg)
Predicates involving multiple columns or non-constant values
Chained window operators with incompatible sort requirements
Global row_number without partition/order columns (uses Limit instead of TopN)
framework_notes: Matches LogicalFilter -> LogicalWindow pattern. Checks session variable for pipeline engine. Transforms to LogicalFilter -> LogicalWindow -> LogicalTopN. Validates sort group compatibility with downstream window operators to prevent negative optimization gains from extra enforcers.

summary: This rule optimizes Logical Aggregation operators by removing redundant columns from the GROUP BY clause when they are functionally dependent on other grouping columns due to Unique Key (UK) or Foreign Key (FK) constraints.
ra_condition: G functionally determines D (G → D) via UK/FK constraints, and D is not required by parent operators
correctness_conditions: Valid Unique Key or Foreign Key constraints must exist and be collected in the optimizer context
The session variable 'enable_uk_fk_opt' must be enabled
Pruned columns must not be required by the parent operator (verified via requiredOutputColumns)
Expressions in GROUP BY must be deterministic and depend on a single UK column if pruned
The transformation preserves semantic equivalence of the grouping operation
performance_impact: Reduces aggregation cardinality, hash table memory usage, and network shuffle data volume. May incur minor overhead for constraint collection and validation.
edge_cases: Queries where UK/FK constraints are not defined, trusted, or collected
Group By columns explicitly required by the parent query block
Non-deterministic functions used in Group By expressions
Nullable columns where UK constraint semantics might allow NULLs affecting FD
Aggregation types that rely on specific grouping key structures (rule forces GLOBAL type)
framework_notes: Matches LOGICAL_AGGR over LOGICAL_PROJECT pattern. Collects constraints as a side-effect in the check phase. Forces AggType to GLOBAL in the transformed operator. Does not trigger physical enforcers directly.

summary: This rule visits LogicalCTEConsume operators to collect consumption metadata (predicates, limits) into the CTEContext without altering the logical plan structure, enabling subsequent CTE optimization decisions.
ra_condition: Operator type is LOGICAL_CTE_CONSUME
correctness_conditions: CTE ID must be registered in the optimizer context
Column reference mapping from consume to produce must be valid
Predicate rewriting must preserve semantic equivalence
performance_impact: Negligible direct cost; enables significant performance gains by facilitating predicate pushdown into CTEs and informed materialization vs. inlining decisions.
edge_cases: Recursive CTEs may require special handling not covered here
CTEs consumed multiple times aggregate limits/predicates from all sites
CTEs with no consuming nodes (dead code) are not processed by this rule
framework_notes: Returns empty list (no plan alternatives generated); relies on side-effects to update OptimizerContext's CTEContext. Matches group expressions containing LogicalCTEConsumeOperator. Does not trigger enforcers.

summary: Splits a Logical Limit operator into a Global Limit wrapping a Local Limit to optimize data transfer in distributed execution plans.
ra_condition: limitOperator.isInit() returns true
correctness_conditions: Local limit count (L + O) must be >= Global limit count (L) + Global offset (O)
Distributed execution semantics must ensure local limits are applied per shard before global merge
Ordering requirements (if any) must be preserved through the local-global split
performance_impact: Significantly reduces network traffic in MPP environments by filtering rows at scan nodes; minimal CPU overhead for extra operator node.
edge_cases: Limit value is 0 (returns empty set immediately)
Input cardinality is less than Offset (returns empty set)
Single-node execution where distribution split provides no benefit
framework_notes: RBO Logical Transformation. Matches LogicalLimit on any child. Does not directly insert Enforcers but tags operators for PhysicalPlanner fragment assignment.

summary: Eliminates constant columns from the GROUP BY clause when an Aggregation operator is preceded by a Project operator that defines those columns as constants, reducing grouping cardinality and hash key size.
ra_condition: G ∩ constants(P) ≠ ∅ AND |G| > 1 AND session.enable_constant_execute_in_fe = true
correctness_conditions: Session variable 'enable_constant_execute_in_fe' must be enabled
Aggregation must have more than one grouping key initially (|G| > 1)
At least one grouping key must be mapped to a constant scalar in the child Project
If all grouping keys are constant, one key is retained to preserve grouping structure
Column references in aggregations and predicates must be rewritten to match new projection map
performance_impact: Beneficial: Reduces memory consumption and CPU cost for hashing/comparison by shrinking group by keys. Detrimental: May introduce additional Project operators if non-constant projections exist, adding minor overhead.
edge_cases: Queries with only one GROUP BY key (rule explicitly skips)
Queries where all GROUP BY keys are constants (one key kept to avoid empty group list)
Session variable 'enable_constant_execute_in_fe' is disabled
Aggregation operator is not directly preceded by a Project operator
Grouping keys not found in Project's column reference map
framework_notes: Matches LOGICAL_AGGR over LOGICAL_PROJECT pattern in StarRocks Cascades memo; rewrites OptExpression tree using ReplaceColumnRefRewriter; does not trigger physical Enforcers directly.

summary: Rewrites COUNT(DISTINCT bitmap_column) aggregations to BITMAP_UNION_COUNT(bitmap_column) to leverage specialized bitmap processing logic for better performance.
ra_condition: type(c) == BITMAP ∧ func == COUNT ∧ isDistinct == true
correctness_conditions: The column c must be of BITMAP type
COUNT(DISTINCT bitmap) is semantically equivalent to BITMAP_UNION_COUNT(bitmap) in StarRocks
The aggregation operator must support function replacement
Single argument to COUNT function
performance_impact: Highly beneficial for bitmap columns; BITMAP_UNION_COUNT uses efficient bitwise operations and bitmap serialization instead of generic hashing or sorting required for standard DISTINCT counting.
edge_cases: Non-bitmap column types
COUNT without DISTINCT modifier
COUNT with multiple arguments
Aggregations already using BITMAP_UNION_COUNT
framework_notes: Matches LogicalAggregationOperator with leaf children. Forces AggType to GLOBAL in the transformed operator regardless of input AggType. Uses GlobalStateMgr to resolve the target function signature.

summary: This rule pushes filter predicates down through join operators to reduce data volume earlier in query execution. It matches a LogicalFilter on top of a LogicalJoin and distributes predicates to appropriate join sides based on column references.
ra_output_pattern: σ_p1(R) ⋈_q σ_p2(S) where p ≡ p1 ∧ p2
ra_condition: Predicate p references columns from R and/or S; for OUTER JOIN requires context.isEnableJoinPredicatePushDown()
correctness_conditions: Predicates must only reference columns available from join inputs before join execution
For OUTER JOIN, predicates on non-preserved side must not filter out null-extended rows incorrectly
Join predicate pushdown must be enabled in OptimizerContext for outer join cases
Predicate expressions must be semantically equivalent when evaluated on reduced input sets
performance_impact: Generally beneficial as it reduces join input cardinality early, decreasing memory and CPU usage. May hurt performance if predicate evaluation is expensive and gets duplicated across both sides, or if it prevents vectorized batch processing optimizations.
edge_cases: Outer joins with predicates referencing non-preserved side columns may produce incorrect results if pushed incorrectly
Predicates referencing columns from both join inputs simultaneously cannot be fully pushed down
When context.isEnableJoinPredicatePushDown() returns false for outer joins, rule is skipped
Complex expressions or correlated subqueries in predicates may limit pushdown opportunities
framework_notes: Matches GroupExpressions with LogicalFilter as parent of LogicalJoin in Cascades memo. Uses JoinPredicatePushdown helper class to analyze predicate column references and determine placement. Does not directly trigger Enforcer insertion. Optimization depth controlled through OptimizerContext configuration flags.

summary: This rule rewrites a single-stage logical aggregation containing COUNT DISTINCT into a two-stage aggregation (local pre-aggregation followed by global aggregation) to optimize distributed execution performance by reducing network shuffle traffic.
ra_condition: R is Hash Distributed, ShuffleKeys(R) ⊆ (G ∪ {D}), ShuffleKeys(R) ⊈ G, D is single column reference, agg functions are supported
correctness_conditions: All distinct aggregate functions must be in the supported transformation map (e.g., COUNT, SUM)
All non-distinct aggregate functions must be decomposable into 1st and 2nd stage functions (e.g., COUNT->SUM)
The distinct expression must be a single column reference
